sections_scanned: Abstract; methodology; training/evaluation plots; conclusion/further research
gold_standard_models: MuZero with self-competition and tree search (clustered CPP decomposition)
performance_metric: Success rate, search-time practicality, and training convergence signals (reward/value losses)
best_score_or_gain: qualitative: high success rates and reasonable search times reported for complex networks
technological_sota: Model-based RL (MuZero) for autonomous CPP decision-making in large SDN topologies
methodological_sota: Markov-game framing with self-play/self-competition and iterative policy/value optimization
functional_sota: Can autonomously identify strong controller placements and improve placement decisions in complex environments
main_limitation: Need broader real-world validation and environment-specific adaptation despite strong simulation outcomes
sota_statement: SotA in this paper is AI-native autonomous CPP solving via MuZero-driven planning over complex placement spaces.
gap_statement: Generalization and operational validation under diverse production constraints remain the key unresolved gap.
evidence_quote: The implementation of the MuZero reinforcement learning algorithm coupled with self-competition demonstrates its effectiveness in optimizing the placement of controllers in large networks.

domain: SDN controller placement (deep RL + adaptive clustering)
sections_scanned: Abstract; related work; AP-DQN method; multi-topology evaluation; sensitivity analysis
gold_standard_models: AP-DQN (improved Affinity Propagation + DQN); comparisons include MODECP, Multi-GA, Random-CP, ReCAP
performance_metric: Load balancing, latency, link operating cost, normalized security
best_score_or_gain: 24% load-balance improvement; 25% latency reduction; 28% lower link operational expense; 12% higher normalized security
technological_sota: Hybrid unsupervised clustering plus deep RL policy optimization for multi-objective CPP
methodological_sota: Formulates CPP as MDP, initializes candidate zones via improved AP, then trains DQN with reward shaping and weight sensitivity checks
functional_sota: Improves dynamic multi-objective placement quality across network topologies and failure/traffic conditions
main_limitation: Complex criterion design and dynamic-network assumptions still challenge interpretability and transferability
sota_statement: SotA is an integrated clustering-plus-deep-RL placement pipeline that jointly optimizes latency, load, cost, and security.
gap_statement: Gap remains in making multi-objective RL decisions fully interpretable and consistently transferable across heterogeneous operational deployments.
evidence_quote: Simulation results show that AP-DQN can obtain 24 % improvement in load balancing, 25 % reduction in latency, 28 % reduction in link operational expenses, and 12 % improvement in normalized security level compared to comparative techniques.

sections_scanned: Abstract; Internet2 OS3E analysis; 100+ WAN topology analysis; conclusion
gold_standard_models: Foundational CPP latency optimization formulations (average vs worst-case latency tradeoff)
performance_metric: Random-vs-optimal latency ratio, latency-bound feasibility, diminishing returns with added controllers
best_score_or_gain: random placement about 1.4x-1.7x worse average latency and up to 2.5x worse worst-case; one-controller 50 ms feasibility for 82% topologies
technological_sota: Early formalization of controller count/location tradeoffs under latency constraints across diverse WAN topologies
methodological_sota: Exhaustive/optimized placement comparison on real topologies with explicit latency-bound target analysis
functional_sota: Shows many networks can meet strict reaction-time bounds with one well-placed controller while optimized placement significantly outperforms random
main_limitation: Fault tolerance/availability remains outside pure latency-optimal placement and requires additional design objectives
sota_statement: This seminal SotA work established that CPP outcomes are topology- and metric-dependent, with strong benefits from optimized placement over random selection.
gap_statement: Latency feasibility alone does not solve availability and resilience, so practical deployments require multi-objective extensions beyond this baseline.
evidence_quote: More surprisingly, one controller location is often sufficient to meet existing reaction-time requirements (though certainly not fault tolerance requirements).

sections_scanned: Abstract; Related Work; Results (Sec 6.3); Conclusions and Future Work
performance_metric: Propagation latency; average delay; reliability; throughput; fitness
best_score_or_gain: 11-20 ms latency (vs 22-40 and 27-45); reliability 0.88 +/- 0.1; throughput 197.1 +/- 0.1; fitness 22.5
technological_sota: Hybrid meta-heuristic controller placement combining Harmony Search and PSO for multi-controller SDN
methodological_sota: CloudsimSDN simulation with metric-by-metric baseline comparison across iterations/switch counts
functional_sota: Can jointly improve latency, reliability, throughput, and placement fitness in simulated multi-controller SDN
main_limitation: Primarily simulation-based validation and algorithm sensitivity to deployment assumptions are not fully resolved
sota_statement: State of the art here is hybrid meta-heuristic placement that outperforms prior heuristic baselines across core control-plane metrics.
gap_statement: Strong simulated gains may not fully transfer to real production SDN without broader topology/traffic operational validation.
evidence_quote: The proposed MC-SDN method achieves low propagation latency (11 to 20 ms) when compared with SA-FFCCPP (22 to 40 ms) and GSOCCPP (27 to 45 ms) methods.

sections_scanned: Abstract; Related Works; Methodology; Results; Conclusion
performance_metric: Controller overhead; packet loss; switch-controller latency; throughput
technological_sota: Critical-switch-aware partitioned placement for SD-WAN with k-means-based partitioning and optimization objective over overhead and delay
methodological_sota: Emulation/simulation-based comparative benchmarking on multiple topologies with shared traffic matrix assumptions
functional_sota: Improves overhead-loss-latency-throughput jointly for distributed multi-controller decisions
main_limitation: Authors note unresolved risks around homogeneous-controller common vulnerabilities and interoperability with traditional IP ecosystems
sota_statement: Current SotA in this paper is critical-switch-aware placement that explicitly optimizes control overhead and latency together while preserving throughput.
gap_statement: Even with improved metrics, security/common-mode failure risks and interoperability constraints remain open for robust deployment.
evidence_quote: The proposed solution has achieved an aggregate reduction in the controller's overhead by 73%, loss by 51%, and latency by 16% while improving throughput by 16% compared to the benchmark algorithms.

sections_scanned: Abstract; Intro/Background; Related Work (CPP surveys); Ch5-Ch7 Results; General Conclusion
performance_metric: Avg. SC latency; SC flow rate; Avg. CC latency
best_score_or_gain: up to 19.89% latency improvement; up to 33.16% flow-rate improvement; up to 55.01% CC-latency improvement
technological_sota: Centrality-driven and meta-heuristic CPP (WBC + GWO + PSO) for latency-reliability-load tradeoffs in dynamic SD-IoT
methodological_sota: Simulation-driven multi-topology benchmarking (Topology Zoo, OS3E, synthetic, population-aware network) with baseline comparisons and reduced candidate search space
functional_sota: Joint optimization of latency, reliability, and load with scalable multi-controller placement strategies in heterogeneous traffic
main_limitation: Generalizability to real deployments remains constrained by simulation assumptions and dynamic traffic complexity
sota_statement: State-of-the-art in this work emphasizes multi-objective, scalable CPP that outperforms conventional baselines on latency/reliability/load metrics.
gap_statement: Performance gains are strong in simulation, but real-world noisy/operational constraints and deployment-level validation remain the key gap.
evidence_quote: The results show notable performance gains in terms of latency, load balancing, reliability, and overall network efficiency when compared to conventional CPP and baseline SDN placement methods.

sections_scanned: Abstract; methodology overview; future directions; conclusions
gold_standard_models: ML/DL/ensemble model families (CNN-LSTM, BiLSTM, tree boosting families such as XGBoost/CatBoost/LightGBM in surveyed studies)
performance_metric: Forecast accuracy and error metrics (accuracy/RMSE) across surveyed TCP studies
best_score_or_gain: survey examples include 91% accuracy, around 11% improvement in a connected-vehicle case, and RMSE 4.42 in a cited deep model
technological_sota: Shift from classical statistical forecasting to data-driven ML/DL/ensemble methods for non-linear traffic dynamics
methodological_sota: Comparative review synthesizing regression/classification metrics, horizons, and data-preprocessing pipeline recommendations
functional_sota: Enables more accurate proactive congestion prediction to support traffic management decisions
main_limitation: Interpretability, scalability, privacy, and deployment-readiness remain open; review notes missing interpretable seq2seq-capable solutions
sota_statement: Current SotA emphasizes hybrid and deep predictive approaches that better capture complex urban traffic patterns than traditional methods.
gap_statement: Despite improved predictive power, practical deployment is constrained by model interpretability, data governance/privacy, and cross-city adaptation gaps.
evidence_quote: Future work must focus on making these models more interpretable, scalable, and adaptable, as well as ensuring robust data privacy.

sections_scanned: Abstract; related work; algorithm section; experimental results; conclusions/future work
performance_metric: Integrated latency; reliability; load variance; integrated placement overhead
best_score_or_gain: vs MOPSO: worst latency about -8%, reliability about +10%, load variance about -20%, overhead about -10%; vs NSGA-II: worst latency about -20%, average latency about -5%, reliability about +7%, overhead about -14%
technological_sota: Improved multi-objective Harris Hawks Optimization for hierarchical SDN controller placement under latency-reliability-overhead objectives
methodological_sota: Simulation benchmarking on three real large-scale topologies (ATT, China Telecom, IRIS) against representative multi-objective baselines
functional_sota: Produces robust multi-controller placement with lower integrated latency and overhead and higher reliability while preserving load balancing
main_limitation: Selection/placement limitations for multi-objective CPP remain and future work is needed for security-oriented dynamic placement with intrusion detection
sota_statement: SotA in this paper is robust multi-objective hierarchical CPP optimization that improves latency-reliability-overhead tradeoffs on large SDN topologies.
gap_statement: Even with better optimization outcomes, practical security-aware dynamic placement and generalization beyond tested scenarios are still open gaps.
evidence_quote: The experimental comparison ... shows that ... the CPP scheme obtained by the MOIHHO algorithm achieves better results in terms of integrated latency, integrated reliability and integrated placement overhead.
